task: I need to get across town after school without messing up my bike, and I’m trying to figure out what I should check before I leave. Can you help me make a quick go/no-go checklist for a bike ride so I don’t end up stranded?

turns.agent_response:
Yep — quick go/no-go checklist:

**Go only if:**
- Tires are firm, no obvious flats/cuts
- Brakes work and stop the bike cleanly
- Chain is on, seated, and not grinding
- Wheels spin straight, nothing rubbing
- Handlebars and seat are tight
- Quick-release/axles are secure
- Gears shift normally, no weird skipping
- No loose parts, cracks, or major damage

**No-go if:**
- Brake feels weak or one side doesn’t work
- Chain keeps slipping, derailing, or making bad noise
- Tire is flat, bulging, or cutting apart
- Wheel wobbles a lot or rubs the frame/brakes
- Anything is loose and you can’t tighten it fast
- You hear grinding, clicking, or metal-on-metal under load

**Last 10-second check:**
- Squeeze both brakes
- Lift and spin each wheel
- Pedal once or twice
- If it feels wrong, don’t force it

If you want, I can turn this into a super short **before-you-roll 30-second checklist**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s fine. Short and usable. The 10-second check is basically what I wanted — brakes, wheels, chain, done. I’d actually add one thing: if the chain already slipped once or the tire looks a little soft, I’m not risking it. Not worth getting stuck halfway.
